actions-list.actionItem: Pursuant to the order of Thursday, March 12, 2026,  the Journal of the proceedings of the Senate was deemed approved to date.
Pursuant to the order of Thursday, March 12, 2026, the morning hour being deemed expired, and the times for the recognition of the two leaders being reserved.
The following transactions of morning business occurred on today.

S.Res. 629 (Sen. Durbin):
        A resolution honoring the life and legacy of Reverend Jesse Louis Jackson, Sr., and commending him for his significant leadership during the Civil Rights Movement and his decades of advocacy in pursuit of justice, equality, and human rights.

        -- Senate Committee on the Judiciary discharged by Unanimous Consent.

        -- Resolution agreed to in Senate without amendment and with a preamble by Unanimous Consent.


 S.Res. 644 (Sen. Young):
        A resolution honoring the life and legacy of Coach Louis Leo "Lou" Holtz.

        -- Submitted in the Senate, considered, and agreed to without amendment and with a preamble by Voice Vote.

PN787-4:
        Anna St. John, of Louisiana, to be United States District Judge for the Eastern District of Louisiana

        -- Considered by Senate.

        -- By unanimous consent agreement, debate and vote 3/17/2026.
By unanimous consent, on the request of Mr. Barrasso, at 4:53 p.m., the Senate adjourned, under its order of today, until 10 a.m. tomorrow.
